item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun fact: This article's main image is on the front page of Wikipedia right now (9/24/19). I recognized it from here immediately.

item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Edmontosaurusmummy.jpg

Public Domain

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ercole_Hydra_76001812.jpg

CC BY-SA 2.5

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting lore connections, I like. Very minor thing, but given that SCP-1050 apparently allowed for the translation of Linear A, shouldn't those scrolls be decipherable by the Foundation if no one else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: {nevermind}

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is where 2406 fought before wenturing towards Aral Sea?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, that means a lot. I am currently busy with my primary writing projects but would love to see others expand upon this in their own way. I wanted to create the outline of a lost narrative in history - but present it with enough room that others could continue it (just as what was done with the CotBG).

Oddly, flesh beasts aren't even my thing normally; it just turned out that way when trying to come up with a literal interpretation of some old alchemical (specifically the Visions of Zosimos) and Gnostic texts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My hesitations about SCP-2480 aside, I really do love the worldbuilding you're doing with these articles. It compliments what's been done with the CotBG and the Daeva excellently in spite of its reliance on your standard 'flesh beast' tropes.

I look forward to seeing more in this continuity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Charred bones suggesting the incineration began internally and I'm pretty sure radiation damage can be gleaned from skeletal material. But if I'm wrong, I'll correct that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has included skeletal remains depicting likely anomalous causes for death such as implosion, internal combustion, disintegration, radiation poisoning, and fatal physical reconfiguration.

How could examination of thousand year old (or more) skeletal remains show evidence of radiation poisoning or internal combustion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pictures are broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will correct that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a quibble, but the texts shouldn't be described both as scrolls and codices, they're two different things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the curse tablets in the inventory list should be placed around the perimeter of the island. Not sure if it's ok to edit that myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's all very confusing. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not saying it is and I'm not saying it isn't. I've had an idea for a GoI similar to this for some time and a connection to the Flesh That Hates is up for others. The underground structure was a single organism (albeit mindless) "grown" to act as a temple by a people who revere flesh and organic matter.

The Flesh That Hates is also in Russia, if I recall correctly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, "strewn carelessly" is not very clinical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure this adds much. I'm also not a fan of using a skip to explain where another skip came from (someone upthread suggested this is the origin of the Flesh That Hates). Maybe it's just that they're both underground structures on Greek islands making them feel too similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how this ties in with 2217, but from the Flesh's side. 610 was something that really needed fleshed out (that was not intentional) origin-wise.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is meant to be related. I wanted to roll the ball some more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. My biggest fear is often getting downvoted for simply not matching someones tastes rather than quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So wait, is this related to the Broken God v. Flesh That Hates skip posted recently? Because all I could think was that it felt very similar. Like, similar enough that we don't need both, or else this should be related to that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I definitely think the categorization of Safe and the containment procedures are fitting. It's just not my style of storytelling is all - but definitely room in the Foundation for all sorts of tales and objects and you're certainly getting a positive response from others, so more power to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That is why it is labeled safe and has fairly reasonable containment procedures. It is more or less a giant mummy at this point.

Can't please everyone, I've learned. As much as I wish I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fixed 2 grammatical errors in description: a missing verb ("is") and changing cellularity to cellularly, as you needed an adverb where it was.

I don't know enough CotBG to get the connections mentioned by the others, though having read this article I really wish I did. I don't really feel any threat or current anomalous effects/nature to this site, other than the fact that broad public knowledge of its existence would of course challenge science and history as we know it. Finally, it's long and feels a bit bulky compared to what it describes. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Superb attention to detail and very intriguing. Good job, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

Oh my god. This is the origin of the Flesh That Hates. And do I sense a connection with the Heart of Darkness as well?

All my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very ominous and creepy. I like it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been working on some other things to suggest this group remained quite active in Eastern Europe, Russia and so on:

NSFW due to the dead in the backdrop:
http://visual-records.wdfiles.com/local--files/phenomenon/ritual.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I spent the entire article wondering "Is this about the Broken God?"

I was not disappointed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are totally write. That was actually a minor cleanup done fight before posting but apparently still got it wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this, but there are slip-ups in clinical tone. One specific example that stands out is "no doubt." This is speculative history. Knowledge is constantly evolving: we currently think humanity is 100,000 years old, give or take. A week from now, an archaeologist might find evidence of modern humanity 900,000 years old. There's definitely doubt as to why the letter wasn't sent. There are others like it, too.

That being said, I upvoted.
